result.preview: "I\u2019m unable to identify which patients are LGG (Brain lower grade glioma) from the available clinical_info table, because it does not include a cancer-type or project/acronym column such as Cancer_type_acronym, Study, or Project, and only generic fields like histological_type, tumor_tissue_site, and diagnosis are present. Without a reliable way to subset the clinical data to LGG patients, I cannot correctly restrict IGF2 expression to LGG cases and therefore cannot compute the requested LGG-specific averages by histology type.\n\nIf you can provide either (1) the name of the column in clinical_info that encodes LGG vs other cancer types, or (2) a separate mapping of patient barcodes to cancer type, I can then join this with the IGF2 expression data, filter out histology strings enclosed in square brackets, compute log10(normalized_count + 1) for valid IGF2 values, and report the mean for each histology type to at least four decimal places."

args.answer:
I’m unable to identify which patients are LGG (Brain lower grade glioma) from the available clinical_info table, because it does not include a cancer-type or project/acronym column such as Cancer_type_acronym, Study, or Project, and only generic fields like histological_type, tumor_tissue_site, and diagnosis are present. Without a reliable way to subset the clinical data to LGG patients, I cannot correctly restrict IGF2 expression to LGG cases and therefore cannot compute the requested LGG-specific averages by histology type.

If you can provide either (1) the name of the column in clinical_info that encodes LGG vs other cancer types, or (2) a separate mapping of patient barcodes to cancer type, I can then join this with the IGF2 expression data, filter out histology strings enclosed in square brackets, compute log10(normalized_count + 1) for valid IGF2 values, and report the mean for each histology type to at least four decimal places.

val_args.answer:
I’m unable to identify which patients are LGG (Brain lower grade glioma) from the available clinical_info table, because it does not include a cancer-type or project/acronym column such as Cancer_type_acronym, Study, or Project, and only generic fields like histological_type, tumor_tissue_site, and diagnosis are present. Without a reliable way to subset the clinical data to LGG patients, I cannot correctly restrict IGF2 expression to LGG cases and therefore cannot compute the requested LGG-specific averages by histology type.

If you can provide either (1) the name of the column in clinical_info that encodes LGG vs other cancer types, or (2) a separate mapping of patient barcodes to cancer type, I can then join this with the IGF2 expression data, filter out histology strings enclosed in square brackets, compute log10(normalized_count + 1) for valid IGF2 values, and report the mean for each histology type to at least four decimal places.
